# Research

This page highlights research projects involving biostatistics, clinical research, statistical modeling, and applied data analysis.

::: {.research-grid}

::: {.research-card}
## The Effect of Cervical Disc Replacement Fixation Features on Device Stability

**Role:** Biostatistics Research Intern  
**Tools:** R, RStudio, Excel  
**Focus:** Clinical research, medical devices, statistical analysis

Analyzed cervical disc replacement devices to evaluate how fixation features and device attributes contributed to stability and patient-related outcomes. This work involved data collection, statistical analysis, visualization, and interpretation of complex medical data.

**Methods used:** regression, t-tests, ANOVA, Taguchi’s method, data visualization

**Outcome:** Co-authored a manuscript submitted to *The Spine Journal*.
:::

::: {.research-card}
## Effect of Median Age on Total Health Spending

**Tools:** R, RStudio, GitHub  
**Focus:** Regression analysis, health economics, statistical modeling

Collaborated on a statistical analysis examining how median age relates to total health spending across countries. The project used regression modeling to explore population-level trends in healthcare expenditures.

**Methods used:** regression analysis, data visualization, statistical communication

[View GitHub Repository](https://github.com/dle-79/stat331-final-project)
:::

:::
